questions.question: Which country is the host of Para Archery Asia Cup 2025?
questions.explanation: The Indian para-archery team won six gold medals at the 2025 Para Archery Asia Cup – World Ranking Tournament. India topped the medals tally with a total of 12 medals, including six gold, three silver, and three bronze. The tournament took place in Bangkok, Thailand, from February 2-11, 2025.

questions.question: Which lake in Rajasthan has been renamed as Varun Sagar Lake?
questions.explanation: Ajmer municipal corporation renamed Foy Sagar Lake to Varun Sagar and King Edward Memorial to Maharishi Dayanand Memorial Vishranti Grah. Foy Sagar Lake in Rajasthan was built in 1892 as a famine relief project by the English engineer Foy. Until 1995, it was Ajmer’s main drinking water source before the Bisalpur Dam was constructed. The lake was neglected but revived last year to supply water to nearby regions.

questions.question: Recently, the oldest Jurassic bird fossil has been found in which country?
questions.explanation: The oldest known Jurassic bird fossil was recently found in China’s Fujian Province. The fossil is of a species called Baminornis zhenghensis and is estimated to be 149 million years old. The discovery of Baminornis zhenghensis is considered a landmark discovery and one of the most important bird fossils ever found. It shows that birds and dinosaurs lived alongside each other in China during the Jurassic period.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the Clean Mobility Policy 2025-2030 in February 2025?
questions.explanation: Karnataka launched its Clean Mobility Policy 2025-2030 to boost the EV sector with ₹50,000 crore investments and create 1 lakh jobs. The policy aims to make Karnataka Asia’s top clean mobility hub with incentives like 25% subsidy on investments and 20% for Bengaluru. Karnataka has 2.5 lakh EVs and 5,403 charging stations, ranking 3rd in India for EV adoption. It plans 2,600 more charging stations and 3 mobility clusters for innovation. The policy supports training programs and aims to reduce carbon emissions while improving air quality.

questions.question: Which company won the Forward Faster Sustainability Award 2025 in the Water Resilience category?
questions.explanation: NTPC Ltd. won the Forward Faster Sustainability Award 2025 in the Water Resilience category. The award was presented by UN Global Compact Network India (UN GCNI) in Chennai on February 13. NTPC was recognized for its water conservation and sustainable water management initiatives. The award aligns with UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). NTPC follows Reduce, Reuse & Recycle principles and uses advanced wastewater treatment. It promotes clean water access, restores water bodies, and raises conservation awareness. The company sets industry benchmarks in water efficiency and sustainability.

questions.question: Which country has recently approved the construction of a deep-sea research facility in the South China Sea?
questions.explanation: China has approved the construction of a deep-sea research facility in the South China Sea. This facility, also known as a “deep-sea space station”, is expected to be operational by 2030. The facility will be located 2,000 meters (6,560 feet) below the ocean’s surface. It will be one of the deepest and most technologically advanced underwater installations ever attempted. The facility will study cold seep ecosystems, which are methane-rich hydrothermal vents. It will accommodate six scientists for missions lasting up to a month.

questions.question: Exercise Komodo 2025 is organized by which country?
questions.explanation: Indian Navy’s INS Shardul and P8I aircraft are in Bali, Indonesia, for the International Fleet Review (IFR) 2025 and Exercise Komodo. Exercise Komodo, launched in 2014, is a non-combat multilateral naval drill by the Indonesian Navy. It promotes maritime cooperation and regional security among friendly nations. The 2025 theme is “Maritime Partnership for Peace and Stability.” The fifth edition features fleet reviews, naval drills, officer exchanges, bilateral meetings, and a defense exhibition. It involves 39 countries, with 34 foreign and 18 Indonesian Navy warships participating.

questions.question: Which ministry is responsible for organizing the Prime Minister’s Yoga Awards?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Ayush has opened nominations for the Prime Minister’s Yoga Awards for International Day of Yoga (IDY2025). The awards recognize individuals and organizations for their significant contributions to promoting Yoga nationally and internationally. It was established to honor Yoga’s impact on society, the awards celebrate its role in disease prevention and health promotion. Winners in four categories (National/International Individual and Organization) will receive a trophy, certificate, and Rs. 25 lakh cash. Nominees must be at least 40 years old with 20 years of service in Yoga promotion. A screening committee will review applications, and an evaluation jury will make final decisions.

questions.question: The sixth edition of Exercise Dharma Guardian is conducted between India and which country?
questions.explanation: The sixth edition of Exercise Dharma Guardian, a joint military exercise between India and Japan, is scheduled to take place at Mount Fuji, Japan from February 25 to March 9. The exercise focuses on enhancing interoperability between the two forces. It involves joint urban warfare and counter-terrorism operations under a UN mandate. The aim is to strengthen military ties through improved interoperability, joint operations, and the exchange of special operations tactics. The exercise helps promote military-to-military relations between India and Japan.

questions.question: Which government department has initiated the NAKSHA programme for urban land survey?

options.label: D
options.text: Department of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan to inaugurate the National Geospatial Knowledge-based Land Survey of Urban Habitations (NAKSHA) program in 152 Urban Local Bodies across 26 states and 3 UTs in Raisen, Madhya Pradesh. The program, led by the Department of Land Resources, aims to update land records in urban areas for better documentation and dispute reduction. It will enhance urban planning, improve ease of living, and promote transparency in property records. Survey of India is the technical partner, conducting aerial surveys. The project will cost ₹194 crore, fully funded by the Government of India.
